_comment: Per-scene golden hashes + Hamming-distance tolerances for the visual gate. Goldens are captured on the OS that CI runs on (currently ubuntu-latest) via the `.github/workflows/visual-recapture.yml` workflow_dispatch job — keeps the verifier (which also runs on ubuntu-latest) at 0 Hamming distance from the golden, leaving the toleranceBits budget purely as headroom against real drift instead of half-spent on a cross-platform OS-rendering delta. toleranceBits is calibrated from observed CI evidence; see scene metadata for sourcing.

bothy-idle-seed-42.toleranceSource: Originally calibrated from 4 Linux CI runs (26350318616, 26350764592, 26351054004, 26351253798) which all reported distance=3 against the prior Windows-captured golden — that was a stable cross-platform rendering delta, not jitter. Re-capture on Linux closes that gap (Linux ↔ Linux verifier now reads 0). Tolerance retained at 8 bits as headroom against future Windows-dev contributor capture (3-bit Windows ↔ Linux delta in the mirror direction) + any future real animation jitter. Re-tighten with evidence; loosen ONLY for a verified true-positive.
